perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be one (1) year unless terminated earlier in accordance with the terms of this Agreement (the "Term").
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall be one (1) year unless terminated earlier in accordance with the terms of this Agreement (the "Term"). Notwithstanding the foregoing, for collaborative projects initiated in the final quarter of the Term, the term shall automatically extend until the completion of said project.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction to the definition of "Term." Initially, the term is defined as one year unless terminated earlier. The added clause introduces an extension for collaborative projects, effectively creating two different definitions of "Term" within the agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction, in particular affecting any section relying on a fixed one-year duration. The contradiction particularly affect the Early Termination section. This means the Term can be one year, unless a project extension takes place, adding confusion in the early termination section because a notice of termination can be written by the first party but depending on the 'project's duration' the agreement can or cannot be terminated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties may terminate its performance of related obligations under this Agreement within thirty (30) days of receipt by the Party of written termination notice.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines the term of the agreement as one year. The modified text introduces an exception where the term can be extended for collaborative projects, thus creating two different definitions of 'Term'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which allows termination with 30 days notice. If the term is automatically extended, the 30-day termination notice may not be effective during the project extension period, creating a direct and impactful conflict about when termination is permissible. This directly violates the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree to develop a list of target cooperation projects and common goals, and consequent agreements if required, within six (6) Months of signing this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree to develop a list of target cooperation projects and common goals, and consequent agreements if required, within twelve (12) Months of signing this Agreement. However, if either Party fails to submit any proposed cooperation project or goal within the initial six months, the period is automatically reduced to three months starting from the end of that sixth month.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the timeframe for developing a list of target cooperation projects and common goals. Initially, it's six months. The added text modifies this to twelve months but also sets forth a three-month period contingent on initial failures, creating potential dispute on what is the real time period for 'target cooperation projects'. This creates uncertainty and an in-text contradiction since there are two different 'period', affecting the 'Obligations of the Parties' that depends on that. This means the agreement may or may not be binding depending on which part of the agreement the parties choose to refer and under which definition and timeline it should take effect
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.  Obligations Of The Parties 3.1. HPIL ET's obligations under this Agreement are to: (i) Follow up on developments regarding GINARES; (ii) Work with GINARES as appropriate. 3.2. GINARES's obligations under this Agreement are to: (i) Keep HPIL ET aware of developments regarding GINARES; (ii) Work with HPIL ET as appropriate.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a contradiction regarding the timeframe for developing a list of target cooperation projects and common goals. Initially, the agreement specifies a six-month period. The changed text alters this to twelve months but adds a contingency for a three-month period based on initial failures. This creates ambiguity and an in-text contradiction because multiple periods are described. This ambiguity impacts the 'Obligations of the Parties', referenced in 'Contradicted Text', because HPIL ET's and GINARES's obligations depend on a clear timeline for project development. Therefore, there's uncertainty about when these obligations come into effect, satisfying the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: All notices and other communications required or permitted hereunder shall be in writing and shall be deemed to have been duly given (a) when delivered in person, (b) five (5) business days after being sent by registered or certified mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid, (c) when dispatched by electronic facsimile transmission (with confirmation of successful transmission), or (d) one (1) business day after having been dispatched by an internationally recognized overnight courier service, in each case to the appropriate Party at the address or facsimile number specified below:
perturbation.changed_text: All notices and other communications required or permitted hereunder shall be in writing and shall be deemed to have been duly given (a) when delivered in person, (b) ten (10) business days after being sent by registered or certified mail, return receipt requested, postage prepaid, (c) when dispatched by electronic facsimile transmission (with confirmation of successful transmission), or (d) one (1) business day after having been dispatched by an internationally recognized overnight courier service, in each case to the appropriate Party at the address or facsimile number specified below: Notwithstanding the foregoing, any notice regarding termination of this agreement shall only be deemed effective if delivered in person.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies several methods for delivering notices, each with a corresponding timeframe for when the notice is considered 'duly given'. The added sentence restricts notices regarding termination to 'delivered in person', effectively introducing a conflicting definition of 'notice' within the same section. This creates a contradiction because other sections assume all notice types as defined above. This creates uncertainty about which notice rules apply, especially regarding critical issues like termination notices vs general notices. This means a party may argue the termination of the agreement was ineffective if not done in person even though other notice forms, previously stipulated, can be deemed valid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties may terminate its performance of related obligations under this Agreement within thirty (30) days of receipt by the Party of written termination notice.
perturbation.justification: The modified text adds a restriction that termination notices must be delivered in person, while the original clause and the contradicted text allow for other forms of written notice. This creates a direct conflict regarding the acceptable methods for delivering a termination notice, potentially leading to disputes over the validity of termination notices delivered through other means (e.g., mail or courier), because the contradicted text allows for a 30 day period after 'written termination notice' is received. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction as it directly contradicts the previously established methods for delivering notices.
